q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is helpers.run_cmd('ps {}'.format(arg)), which executes a shell command constructed from unsanitized user input, i.e., shell execution.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated; Q1 already selected recorded_call.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; Q1 already selected recorded_call.

mock_guidance.target: Mock helpers.run_cmd to intercept the shell command execution.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the first positional argument (the command string) passed to helpers.run_cmd. Verify that the captured string contains the tainted 'arg' value and confirm the injection is possible (e.g., trailing shell metacharacters).
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a plausible fake string (e.g., an empty string or a stub process listing) to allow execution to continue without invoking a real shell.
notes: The vulnerability is in the command string construction, so capturing the argument before execution is sufficient to prove the finding.
Ensure the mock replaces helpers.run_cmd at the module level where it is imported.
